title: Maryland Signs Longevity Ready Act Into Law, Becomes First State to Legislate Longevity Ecosystem
description: Maryland Governor Wes Moore signed the Longevity Ready Maryland Act (HB 278/SB 113), making Maryland the first state to enact legislation specifically addressing the impact of longevity. The law codifies a 10-year plan with four pillars: building a longevity ecosystem, promoting economic opportunity, preparing residents to afford longevity, and optimizing health, wellness, and mobility. This sets a national precedent for proactive aging policy.

title: Homoharringtonine Validated as Novel Senolytic: FDA-Approved Leukemia Drug Extends Lifespan in Mice
description: Published in Nature Communications, researchers screened 2,150 FDA-approved compounds and identified homoharringtonine (HHT) as a potent senotherapeutic. HHT selectively eliminates senescent cells via HSPA5-mediated apoptosis, reverses diet- and age-associated metabolic dysfunction in white adipose tissue, and extends lifespan in both progeroid and naturally aged mice. Its existing FDA approval for leukemia makes it a strong repurposing candidate for aging.

title: Longevity Biotech ETFs Stage Massive Tuesday Rally: ARKG +7.9%, IDNA +6.6%, XBI +5.3%
description: Longevity and genomics ETFs surged on Tuesday April 15 in a continuation of Monday's rebound. ARKG led with +7.88%, followed by IDNA +6.64%, XBI +5.33%, SBIO +4.43%, IBB +4.36%, and BBH +4.13%. All 11 tracked ETFs posted gains. The rally signals renewed risk appetite in healthcare and biotech amid easing macro fears and strong sector fundamentals.

title: HMGB1 Box A Gene Therapy Reverses Age-Related Proteomic Changes in Perimenopausal Monkeys
description: Fight Aging highlights a study showing that intravenous administration of Box A domain of HMGB1 plasmid reversed age-related plasma proteomic changes in perimenopausal cynomolgus macaques, restoring key proteins like APOE and SHBG toward levels seen in younger adults. The therapy also improved bone health, reduced hyperglycemia, and decreased weight gain over 56 weeks with no adverse effects, offering a novel approach to systemic rejuvenation.

title: David Sinclair Teases April 2026 Launch for Longevity Research Funding Initiative
description: Harvard geneticist David Sinclair posted on X that longevity research is being 'starved of funds' and announced he has assembled a team with a launch date in April 2026. With Life Biosciences' ER-100 Phase 1 trial underway and the broader field reaching $3.74B in Q1 2026 funding, Sinclair's initiative could further accelerate investment at a critical inflection point for aging research.

title: Maryland Becomes First State to Pass Longevity Ready Act Into Law
description: Governor Wes Moore signed the Longevity Ready Maryland Act, codifying a 10-year multisector plan to build a longevity ecosystem, promote economic opportunity, and optimize health for an aging population.

title: Homoharringtonine: FDA-Approved Leukemia Drug Shows Senolytic Activity, Extends Mouse Lifespan
description: Researchers screened 2,150 compounds and identified HHT as a potent senolytic that clears senescent cells via HSPA5-mediated apoptosis, reverses metabolic dysfunction, and extends lifespan in progeroid and aged mice.

title: HMGB1 Box A Gene Therapy Reverses Aging Biomarkers in Monkey Study
description: IV administration of HMGB1 Box A plasmid in perimenopausal monkeys reversed age-related proteomic changes, improved bone health, reduced blood glucose, and decreased weight gain over 56 weeks with no adverse effects.

title: America's Longevity Crisis: Fortune Analysis of the 12.4-Year Healthspan Gap
description: Fortune reports that with the oldest boomers turning 80 in 2026, the US faces a 12.4-year gap between lifespan and healthspan. Only 5% of US homes have basic accessibility features, and 80% of older households lack resources for long-term care.

title: Longevity Biotech Investment Hits $3.74B in Q1 2026, Projected $8-9B Full Year
description: Q1 2026 saw 49 financing events totaling $3.74B (56% increase over Q1 2025). DLT analysis projects $8-9B for full-year 2026, confirming the sector's transition from thesis to mainstream investment opportunity.

title: David Sinclair Announces April 2026 Launch for Longevity Funding Initiative
description: Harvard professor David Sinclair posted that 'longevity research is being starved of funds' and revealed he has assembled a team with a launch date in April 2026 to address the funding gap.

title: Life Biosciences Secures $80M Series D for First Human Epigenetic Reprogramming Trial
description: Life Biosciences, co-founded by David Sinclair, secured FDA approval for Phase 1 of ER-100, an AAV-delivered OSK gene therapy targeting optic neuropathies through partial epigenetic reprogramming.

title: FDA Appears Open to Rejuvenation Technologies Via Disease-Specific Pathways
description: Lifespan.io analysis argues the FDA's clearance of Life Biosciences' ER-100 IND signals openness to rejuvenation technologies when framed as disease-specific therapies with accepted clinical endpoints.

title: Vascular Endothelium Aging Review: Mitochondrial Metabolism as Therapeutic Target
description: New review examines how aging reshapes endothelial cell metabolism and mitochondrial function, proposing mitochondria-driven metabolic deterioration as a key therapeutic target for vascular aging.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Multi-Phase Rapamycin Clinical Study
description: NIH-funded randomized placebo-controlled trial of rapamycin in 84 older adults will evaluate dosing, safety, and biological markers to establish evidence-based guidance for the drug's use in aging.

title: Rapamycin Review: Promising but Standardized Human Biomarkers Still Lacking
description: Comprehensive review finds low-dose intermittent rapamycin appears safe over one year in healthy adults, but current animal data do not yet support it as a reliable intervention for extending human lifespan.

title: Bryan Johnson Drops Rapamycin, Adds Lithium and NDGA to 2026 Blueprint Stack
description: Bryan Johnson's 2026 supplement update includes taking NMN/NR six days per week, removing rapamycin (stopped in 2024), and adding low-dose lithium and NDGA for brain function and cellular support.

title: Sex Differences in Aging Interventions: New Review Highlights Understudied Female Biology
description: New review examines sexually dimorphic lifespan and healthspan effects of dietary, genetic, and pharmacological interventions in rodents, highlighting the need to study X chromosome, gonadal hormones, and ovarian health in aging.

title: Rhonda Patrick Q&A #79: Latest Longevity Supplement and Protocol Discussion
description: Dr. Rhonda Patrick's latest Q&A episode covers NAD precursors (NR vs NMN), NAD IV drips, and evidence-based strategies for boosting NAD for recovery and longevity.

title: UC Berkeley Organ-on-a-Chip Platform Replicates 40 Years of Human Aging in Four Days
description: UC Berkeley researchers unveiled a miniaturized organ-on-a-chip platform that replicates 40 years of human aging in four days using aged human serum on stem-cell-derived tissues, enabling rapid screening of longevity interventions.

title: Single Sauna Session Causes White Blood Cell Mobilization
description: New study shows a 30-minute sauna session causes transient spike in circulating white blood cells, with researchers suggesting this exercise-like immune stimulus may contribute to healthspan benefits.

title: RNA-Lipid Nanoparticle Therapy Enables Heart Self-Repair via ANP Production
description: Columbia researchers developed RNA-lipid nanoparticles encoding Nppa that produce pro-ANP in skeletal muscle, which is converted to ANP by Corin in the heart, reducing scarring and improving function in animal models.

title: Longevity Biotech Crosses From Thesis to Mainstream in 2026
description: Alex Zhavoronkov (Insilico Medicine) argues 2026 is the breakout year for longevity biotech, with real translational science, clearer clinical pathways, and investors treating aging biology as a serious opportunity.

title: US Longevity Gains More Widespread Than Previously Thought, BMJ Open Study
description: University of Wisconsin study published in BMJ Open challenges prior estimates of stagnating US longevity, finding gains across all states for birth cohorts 1941-2000.

title: Senolytics Pipeline 2026: Field Splits Into Academic, Biotech, and Local Delivery Lanes
description: Comprehensive review finds human proof-of-biology exists for senolytics, but the field is splitting into academic first-gen, biotech pipeline, and local delivery approaches. Nature Aging compared 21 senolytic agents.

title: Targeting Longevity 2026 Congress: Aging Reframed as Loss of Biological Coordination
description: The 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity in Berlin reframed aging as progressive loss of coordination between biological systems rather than a single defect to fix, emphasizing mitochondria-microbiota-metabolism dialogue.

title: High-Dose Influenza Vaccine Associated With Lower Alzheimer's Risk in 65+ Population
description: Study of 120,775 patients in Neurology found that high-dose inactivated influenza vaccine was associated with lower Alzheimer's risk in months 1-25 post-vaccination compared to standard-dose in adults 65+.

title: Longevity Sector Valued at $23.2B in 2026, Projected $34.8B by 2030
description: The longevity biotech sector stands at $23.2B in 2026 and is projected to reach $34.82B by 2030 at an 11% CAGR, driven by record Q1 funding and expanding clinical pipelines.

title: PANoptosis in Cardiac Aging: Targeting Integrated Cell Death Pathways Shows Preclinical Promise
description: New review examines PANoptosis -- integrated pyroptosis, apoptosis, and necroptosis -- in cardiac aging, with gene editing and RNAi showing preclinical potential to delay cardiomyocyte loss and fibrosis.

title: On Aging 2026 Conference Opens April 20 in Atlanta: Largest US Multidisciplinary Aging Event
description: The ASA's On Aging 2026 conference takes place April 20-23 at the Hyatt Regency Atlanta, themed 'The Power of Belonging' and focusing on amplifying voices and advancing justice in aging.
